perturbation.original_text: In the event the Recipient is required by law, regulation or court order to disclose any of the Owner’s Confidential Information, the Recipient will promptly notify the Owner in writing prior to making any such disclosure in order to facilitate seeking a protective order by the Owner or other appropriate remedy from t he proper authority. The Recipient agrees to cooperate with the Owner in seeking such order or other remedy. The Recipient further agrees that if the Owner is not successful in precluding the requesting legal body from requiring the disclosure of the Confidential Information, it will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information, which it is legally r equired, and it will exercise all reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the Recipient is required by law, regulation or court order to disclose any of the Owner’s Confidential Information, the Recipient will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information, which it is legally required.
perturbation.explanation: Moving part of the sentence significantly weakens the recipient's obligation to notify the owner of a disclosure requirement and seek a protective order. This is a standard requirement to protect confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 5 of the GeschGehG requires measures to maintain the confidentiality of trade secrets, including notification and cooperation obligations when disclosure is legally mandated. By removing the notification and cooperation requirements, the agreement contradicts the intended legal protection of trade secrets under German law.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text requires the recipient to notify the owner before disclosing confidential information under legal mandate, allowing the owner to seek a protective order. The changed text removes this notification requirement, directly contradicting the measures to maintain confidentiality implied by the GeschGehG, as indicated in the Law Explanation. Although no relevant scraped snippets were provided to further assess this, the removal of the notification obligation weakens the protection of trade secrets, creating a legal contradiction under the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Owner represents that it has the authority to disclose to the Recipient any Confidential Information, which it elects to disclose hereunder. The Owner shall not, however, have any liability or responsibility for errors or omissions in, or any decisions made by the Recipient in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement. Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Placing the confidentiality clause after the disclaimer of liability for errors/omissions from owner obscures the intended relationship. This affects the balance of responsibility and could be construed as limiting the recipient's ability to seek recourse even if the disclosure causes damage.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 276 BGB addresses the obligor's responsibility. By interjecting the disclaimer before the confidentiality obligations, the NDA attempts to limit liability for negligence or intent, which is permissible only to a limited extent under German law. The positioning suggests an attempt to disclaim responsibility for gross negligence related to confidentiality breaches, potentially contradicting the spirit and intention of German law by undermining the obligor's duty of care.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 276 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 276 Verantwortlichkeit des Schuldners (1) Der Schuldner hat Vorsatz und Fahrlässigkeit zu vertreten, wenn eine strengere oder mildere Haftung weder bestimmt noch aus dem sonstigen Inhalt des Schuldverhältnisses, insbesondere aus der Übernahme einer Garantie oder eines Beschaffungsrisikos, zu entnehmen ist. Die Vorschriften der §§ 827 und 828 finden entsprechende Anwendung. (2) Fahrlässig handelt, wer die im Verkehr erforderliche Sorgfalt außer Acht lässt. (3) Die Haftung wegen Vorsatzes kann dem Schuldner nicht im Voraus erlassen werden. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a liability disclaimer immediately before the confidentiality clause. The Law Explanation says that this disclaimer attempts to limit liability for negligence or intent related to confidentiality breaches. § 276 BGB (Snippet 1 and 2) states that a debtor (Schuldner) is responsible for intent and negligence, and the liability for intent cannot be waived in advance. By placing the disclaimer before the confidentiality obligations, the changed contract attempts to disclaim liability for errors or omissions related to confidential information, which could include negligence in handling that information. This positioning could be interpreted as an attempt to circumvent the obligor's duty of care, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice required or contemplated by this Agreement shall be given in writing to the individuals whose names are set forth above either via hand delivery or by confirmed facsimile transmission or by Federal Expr ess or comparable overnight carrier.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall also certify to the Owner the timely destruction of all copies of Confidential Information and all notes and memoranda, in whatever form maintained, incorporating any Confidential Information. Any notice required or contemplated by this Agreement shall be given in writing to the individuals whose names are set forth above either via hand delivery or by confirmed facsimile transmission or by Federal Expr ess or comparable overnight carrier.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the notice requirements to a later section following the clause about certifying destruction of confidential information creates ambiguity. This placement makes it less immediately clear that formal notices are needed for critical actions like terminating the agreement or demanding the return of confidential information, as the section is preceded by destruction requirements. It has been relocated to follow the section about obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 130 BGB dictates when a declaration of intent (Willenserklärung), such as a notice of termination, becomes effective. The declaration must be delivered to the recipient. By placing the notice provision in an unrelated section (destruction of confidential information), it could lead to confusion about the requirements for effective delivery, thereby impeding the legal effectiveness of notices and potentially violating the intent of § 130 BGB by creating doubt over the delivery obligations and requirements.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 130 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 130 Wirksamwerden der Willenserklärung gegenüber Abwesenden (1) Eine Willenserklärung, die einem anderen gegenüber abzugeben ist, wird, wenn sie in dessen Abwesenheit abgegeben wird, in dem Zeitpunkt wirksam, in welchem sie ihm zugeht. Sie wird nicht wirksam, wenn dem anderen vorher oder gleichzeitig ein Widerruf zugeht. (2) Auf die Wirksamkeit der Willenserklärung ist es ohne Einfluss, wenn der Erklärende nach der Abgabe stirbt oder geschäftsunfähig wird. (3) Diese Vorschriften finden auch dann Anwendung, wenn die Willenserklärung einer Behörde gegenüber abzugeben ist. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a structural flaw by relocating the notice requirements to a section concerning the destruction of confidential information. This contradicts § 130 BGB, which states that a declaration of intent (Willenserklärung) becomes effective when it reaches the recipient. By placing the notice provision in an unrelated section, it obscures the importance of proper notice for actions like termination, potentially impeding the legal effectiveness of such notices and violating the intent of § 130 BGB.
